status: post-hoc exploratory extension; the existing held-out outputs and original five-method results were inspected before this protocol was written
purpose: Broaden the frozen 600-question audit with the original CRCV score, every already-recorded scalar baseline, two low-budget sample-consistency scores, and two calibration-trained trace ensembles.

data_and_models.split: the unchanged 300 calibration / 300 held-out question split
data_and_models.generation: no new generation; use the saved greedy traces, three saved stochastic answers, and three saved pair judgments per question
data_and_models.label: unchanged strict normalized accepted-alias match

scalar_trace_audit.selection: report all 24 pre-existing answer-level features; do not select them by held-out performance

lexical_disagreement_3.formula: mean over the three sample pairs of 1 - Jaccard(normalized word-token sets)
lexical_disagreement_3.online_cost: three stochastic generations; no judge calls
lexical_disagreement_3.warning: This is a short-answer lexical adaptation, not a reproduction of every SelfCheckGPT variant.

discrete_semantic_entropy_3.clustering: connect a sample pair when its saved same-answer normalized p(No) is below 0.5; use connected components as meaning clusters
discrete_semantic_entropy_3.online_cost: three stochastic generations plus the same three saved pair judgments used by semantic disagreement
discrete_semantic_entropy_3.warning: The Nature method normally uses ten generations and bidirectional entailment; this three-sample same-answer-judge version is explicitly a budget proxy.

trace_logistic_8.standardization: per-feature mean and population standard deviation from calibration only; scales below 1e-6 become 1
trace_logistic_8.optimizer: full-batch Adam, learning rate 0.03, 800 steps, weight decay 0.01, class-balanced binary cross entropy
trace_logistic_8.selection: no feature or hyperparameter search after aggregate results

trace_tree_depth2.features: the same fixed eight features as trace_logistic_8
trace_tree_depth2.model: deterministic CART-style tree, maximum depth 2, minimum 20 calibration rows per leaf, stable tie breaks
trace_tree_depth2.selection: no depth or minimum-leaf search after aggregate results

secondary_metrics: paired bootstrap AUROC difference versus top-3 token surprise
accuracy after rejecting the highest-risk 10%, 20%, and 30%
evaluation.classification_threshold: maximize macro-F1 on the pooled 300-question calibration split, then freeze
evaluation.slices: pooled held-out and each source dataset separately

literature_positioning.P(True): Kadavath et al., Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know, 2022
literature_positioning.SAR: Duan et al., ACL 2024; discussed but not evaluated because saved traces do not include token-relevance deletion passes
literature_positioning.INSIDE_EigenScore: Chen et al., ICLR 2024; discussed but not evaluated because stochastic-answer hidden embeddings were not saved
literature_positioning.Lookback_Lens: Chuang et al., EMNLP 2024; discussed but out of scope because this benchmark has no grounding passage and the public graph does not expose attention maps

interpretation: Because this is post-hoc, report estimates and uncertainty without claiming a new method is confirmed or state of the art. Results from different papers are not numerically comparable unless model, task, label, sampling budget, and split match.
